---
title: The ecosystem
subtitle: The pieces around the data model, and what each one does
---

BDM is one piece of a set of standards and tools that work together. This
page is the map.

| Piece | What it is | Where |
|---|---|---|
| **Data model** (this site) | The human-facing framework: conventions, concepts, guides, specification | [behaverse.org/data-model](https://behaverse.org/data-model) |
| **Schemas** | The machine-readable source of truth: JSON Schemas, field definitions, validators, changelogs | [behaverse.org/schemas](https://behaverse.org/schemas) · [GitHub](https://github.com/behaverse/schemas) |
| **Studyflow modeler** | Visual editor for study designs (studyflows) | [behaverse.org/studyflow-modeler](https://behaverse.org/studyflow-modeler/app) |
| **Behaverse** | The broader project: cognitive assessments, games, models | [behaverse.org](https://behaverse.org) |

How they relate: this site **defines** the prose conventions and renders
summaries; the schemas repository **owns** every machine artifact (this site
fetches them at build time, pinned by `schemas.lock`); tools consume both.

**Datasets:** a public catalog of BDM-formatted datasets will appear here as
soon as the first reference dataset is published — see the
[roadmap](roadmap.qmd).
